qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: JWT authentication bypass via unverified claims used for JWKS endpoint resolution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes authentication bypass via unverified JWT claims controlling JWKS endpoint resolution. The advisory describes case-sensitive URL-encoded slash handling leading to path interpretation inconsistencies and authorization bypass. These are fundamentally different root causes: the advisory is about URL path parsing of encoded slashes (CWE-178/436), while the finding is about JWT claim verification order (CWE-287).

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SSRF via endpoint URL template rendering with attacker-controlled Request data
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes SSRF via URL template rendering with attacker-controlled request data. The advisory describes case-sensitive URL-encoded slash handling in path parsing. Different vulnerable code (endpoint.go template rendering vs. path/router slash decoding), different root cause, different CWE (CWE-918 vs CWE-178/436).

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Template injection via dangerous Sprig functions enabling DoS and reconnaissance
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes template injection via dangerous Sprig functions in the template engine. The advisory describes case-sensitive URL-encoded slash handling in path routing. Completely different code locations (template.go Sprig function loading vs. URL path decoding), different root cause, different vulnerability class.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: YAML deserialization denial-of-service via untrusted request body parsing
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes YAML deserialization DoS via untrusted request body parsing. The advisory describes case-sensitive URL-encoded slash handling in path routing. Different code locations (yaml_decoder.go vs. URL path decoding logic), different root cause, different vulnerability class (CWE-400/502 vs CWE-178/436).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory describes case-sensitive handling of URL-encoded slashes (%2F vs %2f) in Heimdall's path routing, leading to inconsistent path interpretation and potential authorization bypass (CWE-178, CWE-436). None of the four qualifying findings identify this root cause. The findings describe a JWT authentication bypass via unverified claims (CWE-287), SSRF via URL template rendering (CWE-918), template injection via Sprig functions, and YAML deserialization DoS (CWE-400). These are distinct vulnerabilities in the same repository with no overlap in vulnerable code, root cause, or mechanism with the advisory-described issue.
